seo_title: White Bronze Jewelry: Properties, Care & Why We Use It
meta_description: White bronze is a durable, nickel-free alloy with a cool silver tone. Learn its composition, care tips, and why Moon Raven chooses it for handcrafted jewelry.
intro: White bronze is a modern alloy that mimics the cool, silvery sheen of white gold or platinum but with the warmth and character of bronze. At Moon Raven, we work in white bronze for its subtle, understated glow — a metal that holds detail beautifully and ages with quiet grace. It is not plated; the color runs through the entire piece, so it will never wear off.
properties: White bronze is typically an alloy of copper, tin, and zinc, with no nickel content. Its pale, almost ivory-white color comes from the specific blend of metals. It is denser and heavier than sterling silver, giving pieces a satisfying heft. White bronze develops a soft, slightly darker patina over time, especially in crevices, which adds depth to engraved or textured designs. It is hypoallergenic for most people, as it lacks nickel and other common irritants. The metal is durable and resists corrosion, though it can tarnish slowly in humid or acidic environments.
care: To clean white bronze, use a soft cloth and mild soap with warm water. Dry thoroughly. Avoid chlorine, salt water, and harsh household cleaners, which can dull the finish. For a refreshed shine, use a jewelry polishing cloth designed for bronze or brass. If you prefer the darker patina, simply wipe with a damp cloth and avoid polishing. White bronze is sturdy enough for everyday wear, but remove it before swimming or heavy exercise. For deep cleaning or if the piece becomes heavily tarnished, bring it to a jeweler.
who_chooses: White bronze appeals to those who love the look of silver or white gold but prefer a metal with more weight and a unique, evolving patina. It suits minimalist and geometric designs as well as intricate, nature-inspired motifs. This metal is often chosen by people with sensitive skin who need a nickel-free option. It carries an intent of quiet strength — a material that is both modern and grounded, for those who value craftsmanship over flash.

faq.q: Is white bronze good for jewelry?
faq.a: Yes. White bronze is durable, hypoallergenic, and holds fine detail well. It offers a cool silver tone without plating, making it a long-lasting choice for everyday wear.

faq.q: Does white bronze turn your skin green?
faq.a: It is uncommon. White bronze contains copper, which can cause a green discoloration on some skin types when exposed to moisture and acids, but the alloy's composition minimizes this reaction. Keeping the piece dry and clean reduces the chance.

faq.q: What is white bronze made of?
faq.a: White bronze is an alloy of copper, tin, and zinc, with no nickel. The specific ratios vary by manufacturer, but the blend gives it a pale, silvery-white color.

faq.q: How do you clean white bronze jewelry?
faq.a: Clean white bronze with a soft cloth, mild soap, and warm water. Dry thoroughly. Use a polishing cloth designed for bronze to restore shine. Avoid abrasive cleaners or chemicals.
White bronze is generally hypoallergenic because it contains no nickel. However, individuals with copper sensitivities may still react. Most people with sensitive skin find it safe to wear.
White bronze tarnishes slowly, developing a soft, darker patina over time, especially in recessed areas. This can be polished away or left for an antique look.
